perturbation.original_text: Seller hereby covenants and agrees, that from time to time forthwith upon the reasonable written request of Backhaul or Buyer, that Seller will, at Backhaul's cost and expense, do, execute, acknowledge and deliver or cause to be done, executed, acknowledged and delivered, each and all of such further acts, deeds, assignments, transfers, conveyances and assurances as may reasonably be required by Backhaul or Buyer in order to transfer, assign, convey and deliver unto and vest in Backhaul title to all right, title and interest of Seller in, to and under the Assigned Intellectual Property.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller hereby covenants and agrees to consider, from time to time, a written request of Backhaul or Buyer, and Seller will endeavor, at Backhaul's cost and expense subject to internal approval, to do, execute, acknowledge and deliver or cause to be done, executed, acknowledged and delivered, such further acts, deeds, assignments, transfers, conveyances and assurances as may be considered appropriate by Seller to transfer, assign, convey and deliver unto and vest in Backhaul title to all right, title and interest of Seller in, to and under the Assigned Intellectual Property.
perturbation.explanation: The modification weakens the obligation of the Seller to assist in transferring intellectual property. Replacing 'shall' with 'will endeavor' and adding the condition 'subject to internal approval' makes the action discretionary. Similarly, changing 'as may reasonably be required' to 'as may be considered appropriate by Seller' introduces a subjective standard controlled by the Seller, rather than an objective standard of reasonableness. This can create ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the seller's responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Breach of Contract - Specific Performance - Georgia Law
perturbation.law_explanation: Georgia law (Ga. Code Ann. § 13-6-1) allows for specific performance as a remedy for breach of contract when monetary damages are insufficient. However, this remedy is less likely to be granted when the contract language is ambiguous or the obligations are discretionary. The modified language, by introducing subjective standards and weakening the obligation of the Seller, makes it more difficult for Backhaul or Buyer to obtain specific performance because it would be harder to prove a clear breach of a defined obligation.

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces ambiguity and weakens the seller's obligation to assist in transferring intellectual property, potentially hindering Backhaul or Buyer from obtaining specific performance. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'will endeavor' and adds 'subject to internal approval,' making the action discretionary rather than mandatory. This undermines the enforceability of the obligation. While the provided Georgia Code § 13-6-1 (2020) focuses on damages for breach of contract, the Law Explanation highlights that specific performance is less likely when contract language is ambiguous. By making the seller's actions discretionary and subject to their own approval, the modified text creates a situation where proving a clear breach of a defined obligation becomes difficult, thereby contradicting the intent of ensuring the transfer of rights. The original contract has a clear obligation, whereas the modified contract has ambiguity.
